When the game is working as intended, its a blast. Its got really good core gameplay with the way dribbling, passing and defending come together to create close and intense games. All positions and roles have their own joys to them.

[b] However, all too often the game does not work as intended. [/b] Bluetooth tackles dispossessing you of the ball, phasing through the enemy team when attempting to dispossess them, questionable moments where the other team is capable of claiming the ball before you despite your proximity, and most notable and frustrating is when shots phase right through the goalkeeper. When a game defining moment is decided by jank, it can kill your enjoyment and tilt you to no end. 

[b] Latency and server connection can massively and regularly define whether you have fun.  [/b] This could be tolerable in the free beta tests knowing it could possibly be resolved on full release, but the game being released in its current unstable state is quite upsetting for a paid game with microtransactions within. The game as is feels early access, especially considering promises the devs made regarding crossplay are still unfulfilled. While undoubtedly fun, I would recommend to potential buyers to wait 3-6 months before purchasing.

So, I'm almost 18 hours in as of writing this. In all that time, I've only had one single game crash that whole time. This is going to be a longer one, but it's for understanding what you can expect and where some issues are (again, as of writing this, this might be edited when further improvements are made from the dev team).

Yes, there are still things to iron out, not major things, but stuff like the ball not getting into your possession even though you are literally kicking it in front of you. And when you're canceling the matchmaking queue in the menu, it won't kick you out of the queue, so that needs working out. Could also do for a longer timer after the match if you want to re-queue, you barely get to see your stats before having to hit it, otherwise you'll get kicked back to the menu.

And yes, there are teammates you'll come across that think they are hot s*** and dribble until the sun comes up and never heard of something called a team-based game but those most likely also lose the ball within seconds and don't know how to recover it, so I'd sugguest to PASS THE BALL. If you don't count yourself as a one man army then ignore half of that last point.

There are also a couple issues with desync, most likely when a replay is shown, at least that's when I saw it happening but it's not frequent. Sometimes your level after a match is getting displayed as 0 going towards 1 and cosmetics not taking on the color you've set during customization (like the hats), but I'm sure this will be fixed up soon, hopefully with some of the other visual issues, which also aren't many.

Overall it's pretty fun. The matchmaking is surprisingly balanced (at least what I've experienced in Quick Match). You have 3v3, 4v4 & 5v5 to choose from, Ranked is always 5v5. It's basically Rocket League, without the bulls*** power-ups, aerial flying and not fun movement system. The gameplay is just fun with a good learning curve.

To me, this is better, simpler and more fun if you take the time to figure out what you're doing and when to do what. Playing in a team with friends and properly communicating always helps, so if that's available to you, then fun times can be had.

Uma Análise Irreverente do Futebol que Virou Circo (Com Direito a Carrinho no Juiz e Goleiro Voador)

Meus caros gladiadores digitais das quatro linhas virtuais, preparem-se para uma análise tão caótica quanto uma partida de Rematch após três litrões de guaraná. O jogo, desenvolvido pela Sloclap (os mesmos malucos que fizeram você apanhar feito um bambu em Sifu), chegou chutando a porta do gênero esportivo com uma proposta simples: "E se o futebol fosse um anime de porradaria com bola?" 139.

1. O Visual: Sifu Vestiu Chuteiras e Foi pra Balada Cyberpunk
Os gráficos de Rematch são uma mistura de Capitão Tsubasa com Blade Runner depois de uma dose dupla de energético. As arenas variam de florestas com cachoeiras a estações espaciais, e quando alguém marca gol, o cenário explode em cores como se o estádio fosse um palco do Tropicália 915. Os personagens têm um estilo cel-shaded tão estiloso que até o Ronaldinho (sim, ele está no jogo como skin premium) parece um personagem de Jet Set Radio 16.

Destaque: A customização é tão absurda que você pode criar um jogador com vitiligo, prótese robótica e um moicano que brilha no escuro. Quer jogar de Guts do Berserk chutando bola? Só vai! 1315.

2. A Jogabilidade: Rocket League com Seres Humanos (e Muito Ódio no Coração)
Imagine o FIFA Street e o Rocket League tendo um filho após uma noite de tequila. Rematch é isso:

Sem regras: Carrinho de dois pés? Liberado. Goleiro sair correndo pra fazer gol? Pode. Bater na parede pra tabelar como se fosse um skate? Claro que sim! 1113.

Controles "difíceis": Chutar com R2 parece uma tortura medieval, e os passes exigem mira manual, o que faz todo mundo errar 95% das jogadas no começo. É como aprender a andar de novo... de ponta-cabeça 1315.

Goleiro = Super-Herói: O goleiro aqui é o Homem-Aranha do futebol. Ele pode defender, sair jogando e meter um chutaço do meio-campo que ninguém intercepta. É o poder supremo do caos 13.

3. O Online: Onde a Amizade Vai Morrer (Mas Que Diversão!)
O coração do jogo é o multiplayer, mas prepare-se para:

Matchmaking lento: Esperar uma partida 5v5 é como aguardar o ônibus em dia de greve. Sem cross-play no lançamento, então a fila é mais demorada que série da Netflix 1113.

Toxidade nível Várzea: Jogadores aleatórios fazem gol contra, abandonam a partida ou ficam parados como estátuas. Mas com amigos? Vira uma orgia de risadas e jogadas absurdas 915.

Partidas rápidas: 5 minutos por jogo, e se um time mete 4 gols de vantagem, acaba na hora pela "regra da misericórdia" (ou seja, para de humilhar, cara!) 13.

4. Os Problemas: Bugs, Servidores e a Triste Realidade
Nem tudo são flores (ou chuteiras voadoras):

Gol fantasma: Você defende, mas o jogo conta como gol. Justiça poética das ruas 15.

Servidores instáveis: A conexão oscila mais que ânimo de torcedor do Vasco. E sem cross-play, a base de jogadores fica dividida 1115.

Passe de temporada polêmico: Os cosméticos legais estão trancados atrás de grind infinito ou dinheiro real. Ronaldinho, por exemplo, só sai no cartão de crédito 1316.

5. Vale a Pena? O Veredito do Juiz (Que Também é Jogador)
Se você cansou do EA Sports FC e seu realismo burocrático, Rematch é o antídoto perfeito. É caótico, único e divertido pra caramba — desde que você:

Tenha paciência para os bugs.

Jogue com amigos (ou sua sanidade vai pro beleléu).

Não ligue para microtransactions (ou tenha um rim extra para vender).

Nota final:

Diversão: ⚽⚽⚽⚽ (4/5)

Originalidade: 🌟🌟🌟🌟 (4/5)

Servidores: 🐌 (1.5/5)

Frase de efeito: "Não é um jogo de futebol. É um jogo de caos com uma bola no meio." 913.

E aí, vai encarar o Rematch? Ou vai continuar no FIFA chorando por pênalti não marcado?

Rematch es como si Rocket League y un parque infantil con esteroides tuvieran un hijo hiperactivo. El juego es una locura maravillosa: colores por todas partes, personajes rebotando como si la gravedad estuviera de vacaciones, y un balón que parece tener vida propia (y no siempre buena).

Al principio los controles parecen diseñados por un pulpo en un trampolín: no sabes si vas a saltar, chutar o hacer una voltereta digna del Cirque du Soleil. Pero cuando le coges el rollo... ¡maaaaadre mía, qué gozada! Marcar un gol bien colocado te deja con la misma satisfacción que encontrar una patata frita extra larga en tu bolsa.

Eso sí, hay que decirlo: hay gente que chupa más balón que niño en partido del colegio. Pero bueno, cuando consigues organizar una buena jugada en equipo (o robársela al chupón de turno), es otra cosa. ¡BOF!
I don't know "Rematch" is a "Rocket League" killer but it definitely killed FIFA.  I would love to see something similar that kills NBA 2K next.  ;)

The game is absolutely amazing.

BUT. I cannot recommend it in it's current state UNLESS you have 3+ friends to join your party immediately.

The biggest issue is a TOTAL lack of any kind of moderation tools!

- You CANNOT report a player mid-game.
> EVEN if you want to report post-game, the menu is very hard to find (almost purposefully so.)
>> EVEN if you do find it, and recall the user-tag of the player you want to report, it just forwards you to their steam page.

- You CANNOT block players. (see above)

- You CANNOT mute the chat fully. In that, though you can turn the sound off, messages still appear.

There is no internal moderation either. With no significant penalties to AFK or sabotaging players.

Some other issues that I am more hopeful will be fixed soon:

- In a game called rematch, the rematch button barely works. On three occasions I have decided to interrupt my queue, go do something, only to come back to being AFK in a match where all the other players are (reasonably) pissed at me.

(Not that they can do anything, because there are no methods to kick AFK players. LOL.)

- Server/netcode/framerate/la-di-da. It's fine most of the time. Optimisations are on the way as far as I can tell.

- The toxicity is mounting rapidly. At least on the European servers. (Again. No tools to address that yet.)

I would love for all this to change, because gameplay-wise? Bulletproof. 
The fun factor is off the charts, when you're not playing with complete imbeciles.

It's a fun game overall but it still needs a lot of work. Goalkeeper controls are very unreliable and janky. The only mode you can actually play a game is 3v3 the rest of the modes are just utter chaos and a running simulator. The game should appoint position to each player just like they do with the goalkeeper or otherwise 5v5 is just 10 men running at a ball like monkeys.  And let's not talk about some stupidly broken mechanics like shooting the ball above the goal just to disorient the goalkepper and score an easy goal when the ball bounces back. If you miss a shot you should be penalised not rewarded just by missing on purpose. Also the bladeshot is stupidly OP and needs a nerf or tweak.

Would give a 6/10 right now. With proper changes could be a 9/10 for sure.

Edit: After more than 250 hours invested in this game I can say with confidence that this is not a good game. The idea and concept is very good, the execution is utterly atrocious. Was playing with the hopes that the devs would tackle core issues with the game at the first patch(netcode, air missile/dribbling meta ), instead they fixed nothing. This game needs at least 6 more months of beta testing.

I absolutely love this game, and it is super fun to play.

I am going to give this a non-recommendation for now, as much fun as it is. There are many issues, most of which can be mostly ignored such as:

* Cannot keep pub squad if you want to
* Cannot view previous match results
* Lack of quick command customization (you can't call out incoming defender, for example). Voice chat exists, but not everyone uses it
* Since you cannot view previous match results, you can't see match replays
* Ball latency is super wonky, but you can mostly get used to it except for some jank that happens sometimes.

There are things that absolutely need to change for this game to have proper ranked:

* Team cannot call timeout
* Crashes can happen, and the game cannot be paused leading to 4v5 matchup - good luck to your team if you were the keeper.
* Ranking system seems more like an afterthought. You cannot derank from Silver to Bronze, for example..

The game is super fun, though, and I hope to be able to come back and change this recommendation.

[h3]tl;dr[/h3] The best way to describe this game is by comparing it to an unpolished diamond, yes it has the potential to become something great, but for that to happen a LOT more work needs to be put into it. So... unless you wanna pay 25 euro to play what is essentially an beta of a potentially great game, it might be best to wait a year or two before getting this game *assuming it lives up to its potential and its playerbase doesn't disappear*

[h3]--Time for the in depth review--[/h3]
(I made a short conclusion section at the end as well) 

[h3]Positives[/h3] I don't have much to add on top of what's already been said about this game, its fun, it has a high skill ceiling, easy to learn hard to master, I like its artstyle, its the kind of game you can put lots of hours into it without even realizing.

[h3]Now for the negatives[/h3]
- Bugs & Crashes are quite frequent, so far I got 9 crashes and 5 game-breaking bugs in about 180 games
- MTX in a paid game (big no-no even if its just cosmetics)
- No single-player, given how high the skill ceiling is they should have at least added a simple 5v5 mode against bots with different difficulty options in order to allow the players to practice, as well as simply enjoying the game since in a more relaxed environment.
- Extremely long waiting time between games, yet another reason for a single player mode. The wait time on average was about 5 minutes in between games, and the average game time is also about 5 minutes. Even though at the time of writing this review steam clocked in 30 hours for this game, the actual in game time (which you can see in the game menu) for me is 15 hours. Yes you read that correctly, out of my *currently* 30 hours, 15 hours is just the waiting time to get into the matches. And this was during its first weekend after release, when the player base practically reach its peak.
- The netcode is in shambles 
- Wonky goalkeeper mechanics (you press left/right + dive and instead of diving in that direction sometimes it will dive forward, other times you will be positioned perfectly for a shot while also holding down the defense stance button which should result in your keeper catching the ball, but instead it will just wif by your ear and go inside the net, when you press the slide button its like you're on an ice skate ring and if you don't pay attention you might end up outside of the box resulting in you losing the ball from your hands immediately and allowing the opponent the chance of stealing it from you... just to name a few of the issues)
- Slight position auto-adjusting when you're about to hit a volley, which is a bit disappointing given the skill-based nature of the game.
- Griefers not getting punished, I once played a game in which one of the opponents got mad for some reason (they were 2-1 down) and decided to score 3 own-goals to end the game.... Next game he was in my team. Like surely there has to be a limit for this type of bs behaviour right? Yes sometimes a player will score an own-goal, it happens every once in a while, but 3 in the same game? That should lead to a 1 hour ban at least. They should have a system in place where if player X scored a number of Y own goals in a Z time frame then they get banned for 1 hour, then 2, then 3 etc if it keeps happening.
- There is a "penalty for leaving the game" warning in quick matches, but if u leave the game nothing happens, however this is not a negative as of right now, because players should only get penalized for leaving ranked matches, not quick matches with no stakes involved, but I decided to add it to the negatives list in case they will penalize players in the future for leaving quick matches, reason being there are people with main character syndrome who do everything in their power to ruin the experience (they don't pass, they leave the goal wide open when they are GK, they shot from goal to goal, they try to dribble everyone over and over again)... and fair play to them, they can play however they want, but others should also be allowed to leave the match and find another one that they can enjoy, without being penalized for it.
- No music when you're in the lobby, and I'm talking about actual music not the 3 minimalistic pseudo-lounge soundtracks they put on repeat. I know this is a nitpick, normally I don't even focus on the lobby music, but when you spend as much time in a lobby as u do in this game you start to notice it.
- M+K passing/crossing is trash, if you move your mouse even a nano-meter after you've pressed the the pass button, your toon will do a 180 no-scope flick and send the ball right to your opponents in your own half. They tried to be cute and clever with this feature, probably in their tiny heads they thought players needed this feature in order to execute no-look passes or something like that, but the issue is that its overtuned. They need to create a toggle on/off option for this feature OR add a slider which adjusts the sensitivity of the mouse movement in relation to the direction of the pass, where 100 is what we have now, and 0 is disabled entirely.
- Lack of game modes. You have quick-match 3v3, 4v4, 5v5 + ranked 5v5 and some lazy practice drills and that's it. The ranked mode afaik has no rewards... so here's a great consumer friendly idea, instead of gatekeeping cosmetics behind MTX and passes (in a PAID game) how about you give them as rewards for various ranks in ranked mode eh? or how about a story mode? single player quick matches? more practice drills? Maybe even a online & single player 1v1 panna mode? 

This is all I can think of right now from the top of my head, so in [h3]---conclusion---[/h3] this game despite having moments of brilliance, right now is sketchy (bad netcode, wonky goalkeeper mechanics, position auto-adjustments), bare-bones (only a few game modes, no single-player), has excruciatingly long waiting times in between games, has a semi-toxic community (this is 50-50%, some players are awesome) and it seems there are no consequences for bad in-game behaviour (griefing), crashes & game breaking bugs happen quite often, lack of rewards in ranked mode which is bizzare to say the least, has MTX and last but not least it has one of the worst m+k controls I know of. They present this game as a full release with a 25 euro price tag, when in reality its a beta at best with a MTX shop & pass in it. Scoring bangers in the top bins feels  amazing tho.

This game is fundamentally flawed at a mechanical level and the developers are not competent enough to salvage the game.

This will be the first review I've ever written. The game is horrifically buggy, and every update introduces new bugs, some of which have rendered the game completely unplayable.

The core gameplay itself is very fun. I've been waiting decades for a good football game. Rematch is almost there, but the state of the game on release was awful and the devs keep making it worse.

140 hours after my initial review, I've removed the "be patient, wait for the game to be fixed" sentence at the end. The devs keep pushing patches that make the game feel significantly worse. They keep claiming to have fixed the exploits players are using to cheat at the game, all while making it worse to play for everybody else and not actually fixing many of the exploits.

Queue times for 5v5 quickmatch take up to 10 minutes often and there are still a ton of bugs, and they do not seem to respond to bug reports.

Do not spend your money on this game. Wait for it to go to f2p, which is likely its future as player numbers continue to drop rapidly.

There really should be a way to get a refund when developers keep pushing patches that make the game worse, rather than improve it.

addicting PvP sports game with a really good flow to it. Are there selfish teammates who think they are anime protags? Yes, but that doesn't really detract from the experience much considering both teams will have these people. They are obstacles like any other opponent and they are fairly easy to deal with once you are used to the controls.

Game feels like smooth butter on controllers once you understand all the movement mechanics, and the 10 hours I have currently put in is probably 1% of the total playtime I see myself putting in. Also the hype moments when you predict your enemies and outplay them is just *chef's kiss* unmatched.

If you are looking for your next PvP obsession to lose tons of hours just to grind a meaningless rank in then this is it.

Edit 30 hours in: This game just gets better and better as you rank up and your team mates can actually control the ball. This is one of the most fun and addicting PvP games I have ever played!!!!!!!!

New Favorite game

✅ Les réussites du jeu :

Gameplay super fun et rapide : on rentre vite dans le jeu et les matchs sont nerveux, sans arrêt (pas de hors-jeux, pas de fautes, pas de corner).

Contrôles précis et complets : on contrôle les passes, les tirs, les tacles et même les plongeons en gardien. C’est accessible au début mais il faut de la pratique pour vraiment être bon.

Ambiance et visuel agréable : les terrains sont colorés, l’ambiance est dynamique et on ne s’ennuie pas.

Matchs courts et rythmés : parfait pour faire plusieurs parties rapides, surtout entre amis.

Pas de pay-to-win : les achats sont seulement cosmétiques, donc tout le monde a les mêmes chances en jeu.


❌ Les choses à améliorer :

Pas de cross-play pour l’instant : on ne peut pas encore jouer avec des gens sur console, c’est dommage. Les développeurs disent que ça va arriver.

Quelques bugs et problèmes techniques : parfois la caméra bug un peu, il y a eu des soucis de connexion, mais des mises à jour sont déjà en cours.

En solo, ça peut être frustrant : si vos coéquipiers jouent mal, vous subissez un peu le match.

Peut devenir répétitif : les matchs se ressemblent un peu quand on joue longtemps d’affilée, surtout si le matchmaking n’équilibre pas bien les équipes.

Courbe d’apprentissage : ça peut être un peu dur au début de bien viser, bien défendre, et surtout bien gérer la position de gardien.


🎯 Conclusion :

Rematch est un excellent jeu de foot arcade, simple, efficace et surtout fun. C’est parfait si vous cherchez un jeu rapide et skillé à jouer entre amis. Il y a encore des choses à améliorer (cross-play, équilibrage, quelques bugs), mais le potentiel est vraiment là. Je recommande avec plaisir !

This game is like the phases of Goku's spirit bomb. 

Vs Vegeta: It helps to understand there are going to be REALLY good players who are insane enough to take on your whole team and still score. However if you work together one more goal is all it takes to persevere through this formidable foe. 

VS Frieza: There are times when even pulling together to take on the smartest player can cause a major upset. There is no such thing as a final goal with him, your all dead... but atleast he'll start with the little bald one first.

VS Kid Buu: By now youll have exhausted all efforts to over come this Demonic, Psychotic team. There seems to be no end their skillful onslaught and everyone on your team has done everything possible to stop them. Yet you still manage to bring the game into OVERTIME. Its been 5 minutes...

6 minutes...

10 Minutes... 

37 Minutes... and the match still burns on!! WHAT CAN YOU DO?! Nothings wo- The light finally shines in the sky as the ball phases through the sun. A BICYCLE KICK!! "Bro its our only chance!!" Screams your goalie. As your body starts to flow into its elegant form you realize this was just a kid with insane skills from all his previous matches, he didnt mean any harm he was just doing what he knew best. With your body now in full gear and in contact with the ball, both yours and his cursors meet as if to telepathically send a message to each other. 

"Maybe you'll comeback someday as a better player I hope, I'd like that. Adios! I'll be waiting for you! Waiting for that next... REMATCH!!"

TLDR: Although it's definitely fun at times, do not buy it now. This game needs a lot of help before it's in a reasonable state. Also, if you don't intend to play with friends, maybe avoid this game in general.

[h2] Good Concept [/h2]

[list]
[*]The concept of a manual soccer game that isn't Rocket League is pretty neat.
[*]Great game to play with friends.
[*]The mechanical depth is incredible.
[*]Less restrictive than actual soccer.
[*]Simple concept that pays off well.
[/list]

[h2] MISERABLE Execution [/h2]
[list]
[*]The netcode, in a fully-online game, is so incredibly bad I'm amazed they released it. Not kidding, the wind back system that worked in Rocket League (which only wound back about half a second in seriously bad ping) DOES NOT work here, where it winds back, sometimes, multiple seconds. You could have completed an action already and then the server rolls back to three actions before the one you just completed. Completely unacceptable in an entirely online, PvP game.
[*]The controls are pretty janky. The directional passing, at least, is pretty terrible right now on both controller and KBM - although KBM is in a far worse state. Other controls, primarily involving direction on either stick, are finicky and inconsistent. Contextual controls are fine - where the same button does multiple things depending on the context of the game state - but sometimes those contexts change so rapidly that it's nearly impossible to get the timing.
[*]This game has hidden controls and mechanics. The game does not tell you some things about how the mechanics and controls work. These are fine normally but there are so many that are critical, they really should give you some amount of training on it. Learning the header, which is not included in the standard controls and is context-dependent, is critical and not told to you. Sometimes how possession works is tricky too. Please include tutorials on the header and explain how the control changes based on the context. Others too like multipliers.
[*]Bugs like CRAZY. On release, this game had bugs that even effected the gameplay itself, like the kickoff bug. But, there are still bugs that allow for some really game-breaking exploits (header flying, for example).
[/list]

In general, I can see the appeal but I cannot in good conscience recommend this game to anyone yet. It will need a lot of help to be in a state I would call good.

It has the sauce. If you want to wait until bugs are fixed, I would advise giving Rematch some time to cook. Also, both sides of the monetization of this game's argument are being deceptive about the games many types of currencies. There is a free currency which gets you ugly gear that no one cares about, and there is a premium currency that is reserved for cosmetics people would actually want. Gear that you could get with the free currency can also be bought with the premium currency. The battlepass design is stupid and needlessly confusing. I'd say simplify the battlepass so players who pay for it can get what they want and offer some more cosmetics that can be purchased with the currency you earn from PLAYING, what we currently have feels greedy.

That being said, don't let the premium shop and cosmetics ruin your fun, Rematch as a game is solid and has very fun tech to learn.

Rematch é um jogo de futebol arcade em terceira pessoa com potencial que troca o realismo por ação frenética em partidas 3v3, 4v4 e 5v5. A jogabilidade é intuitiva e viciante, com controles simples que permitem dribles, passes e chutes precisos, exigindo habilidade sem depender de atributos ou assistências automáticas. O sistema de goleiro dinâmico, onde jogadores alternam na posição, é criativo, e os cenários vibrantes, como estádios submarinos, trazem charme visual. O ritmo acelerado, sem faltas ou laterais, e o foco no trabalho em equipe criam momentos empolgantes, com partidas curtas (6 minutos) que incentivam “só mais uma”. A ausência de pay-to-win e o ping para comunicação são acertos, reforçando a diversão. Porém, os servidores instáveis causam lags e desconexões, atrapalhando partidas. A IA inexistente força o modo online, sem opção single-player além do treino. Controles podem ser imprecisos, especialmente no gol, onde a resposta às vezes parece aleatória. O conteúdo é limitado, com poucos modos, e microtransações para cosméticos incomodam num jogo pago. Rematch é divertido e promissor, ideal para quem curte Rocket League sem carros, mas precisa de polimento técnico e mais variedade para brilhar.

Игра вызывает двойственные чувства — с одной стороны, идея и механика мне понравились, а с другой — всё выглядит крайне сыро.
Основная проблема — рассинхроны. Это, наверное, самое точное слово, которое приходит в голову. Иногда кажется, что ты играешь не в онлайн-шутер, а в "посмотри, как я завис" с элементами стрельбы.

Отдельная тема — подбор тиммейтов. Это как русская рулетка, где одна пуля — это адекватный человек, а остальные 99 слотов — боты, потерявшие связь с реальностью.

Если подвести итог:
Идея хороша
Потенциал огромный
А вот реализация пока на уровне альфы
Игра нуждается в серьёзном допиле. Но если разработчики возьмутся за ум — может получиться что-то стоящее.

Оценка: 6/10

P.S. Спустя полгода с релиза, НИЧЕГО не изменилось в лучшую сторону. Если купили, как я , терпим, если думаете о покупке НЕ ВЗДУМАЙТЕ! Пока отзывы не станут хотя бы положительными, в противном случае - игру оставили помирать.

Issues That Need Immediate Attention**
1. Server Problems
-Something feels off with the servers. Maybe it’s the quality or ping issues — but the experience is inconsistent. Please check and improve server performance.

2. Ranked Matchmaking is Broken
-I’m Platinum 1, and I keep getting matched with Silver 2 players. That should not happen in a competitive mode. Ranked needs better matchmaking, based on skill levels that actually make sense.

3. Ranked Queue Structure
-There should be two separate ranked modes:

-Solo/Duo Queue – For fair, skill-based matches.

-Flex Queue (1 to 5 players) – For full teams or casual ranked.
This will help keep competitive play fair and balanced.

4. No “Backpass” Rule
-If a teammate passes the ball back to the goalkeeper, the keeper should not be allowed to use their hands. This rule exists in real football and should be added here for better gameplay.

5. No ‘Avoid Teammate’ Option
-Sometimes you get players who just ruin games. We need a button to avoid matching with certain teammates again — even just 1–2 slots would help a lot.

6. No Proper Report System
Right now, the report button just opens the player’s Steam profile. There should be in-game reporting for things like:

-Scoring own goals on purpose

-Not playing their assigned position

-Griefing or AFK behavior
 
-etc

7. Need a Team and Player finder on the Menu.



* Gameplay Tweaks I Would Suggest
Make the goal slightly bigger (about 10%)

Increase the goalkeeper hitbox slightly to match

Boost should recharge 10% faster when you’re on your own half

Boost + volley shots should feel faster and have less curve, for more satisfying shooting



This game have a potential please dont waste it.

Feedback zu Rematch nach 14 Spielstunden

Nach mittlerweile 14 Stunden Spielzeit möchte ich ein kurzes Fazit ziehen. Insgesamt hat Rematch definitiv Potenzial, aber es gibt auch einige Punkte, die meiner Meinung nach dringend überarbeitet werden sollten.

Positiv:

Performance: Das Spiel läuft durchweg stabil – keine Lags, keine Framedrops. Sehr angenehm.

Gameplay: Das Spielgefühl ist insgesamt sehr flüssig. Bewegungen und Animationen greifen gut ineinander.

Grafikstil: Der Stil gefällt mir wirklich gut – klar, übersichtlich und atmosphärisch passend.


Negativ:

Passen: Das Passsystem ist aktuell leider unzuverlässig. Die Präzision und Logik dahinter wirken inkonsistent – das sollte dringend überarbeitet werden.

Teamplay: Viele Spieler agieren sehr egoistisch, was dem teambasierten Ansatz des Spiels stark entgegenwirkt. Das Matchmaking leidet darunter spürbar.

Sounddesign: Die Footsteps wirken unrealistisch laut, selbst wenn Gegner noch weit entfernt sind – das führt oft zu Verwirrung.

Tutorial: Nahezu alle Spieler scheinen das Pass-Tutorial zu skippen, was die Probleme im Gameplay weiter verschärft. Vielleicht wäre ein verpflichtendes Tutorial hier sinnvoll.

Solo-Queue: Spiele mit Randoms sind oft frustrierend. Ohne Kommunikation und Teamplay geht viel vom Potenzial verloren.

5vs5 Ranked: Für Solo-Spieler ist der Ranked-Modus im 5vs5 kaum spielbar. Ohne ein festes Team ist das Frustrationspotenzial sehr hoch – vielleicht sollte es einen separaten Modus für Solo-Ranked geben oder kleinere Ranked-Formate.


Fazit: Rematch hat ein gutes Fundament und macht Spaß, wenn man mit einem eingespielten Team unterwegs ist. Damit es aber langfristig erfolgreich wird, sollten besonders die Themen Teamplay, Tutorial und das Pass-System Priorität bekommen

As much as I'd love to be writing a positive review for this game, there are far too many problems presenting themselves already.

There's a couple of positives, such as the graphics being visually appealing, and the mechanics for the game are enjoyable. Character customisation adds a little bit of character, which is nice too.

HOWEVER, as pretty as the game is, it cannot hide the fact that this is clearly poorly optimised from launch. My biggest issue is the interface, it doesn't flow well. For instance, I was trying out some of the training while waiting to play with my friends, and there's no way to just repeat the same stage after completing it, which can make learning certain mechanics tiresome and irritating, which put me off actually trying at all.
 
Secondly, I noticed a spelling error in the cosmetics section, under accessories it says "Chocker" instead of "Choker", which I think is poor considering time and effort went into this game. I also believe there is a spelling error on one of the in game tip screens (an ie being mixed up on a word but don't quote me on that, a friend saw that one). I understand that it's just a spelling error and does not affect gameplay whatsoever, but it's still shocking considering how anticipated this game was.

My biggest issue with the game however, is the fact that I was expecting crossplay at launch, my partner plays on Xbox, as do many of my friends, so crossplay games are a big selling point for me as decent games with this feature seem to be few and far between lately. I didn't find out about the fact that crossplay was unavailable at launch until AFTER I had purchased the game. Yes I know it's half my fault for not checking, but then again I only heard about the game through my friends, who said it was crossplay. Then to learn that the company announced that it wasn't finished just hours before the early access launch is disgusting, the fact people have paid MORE to play it early, and for those where crossplay was the selling point for them, is shocking, this should have been announced sooner and more clearly. 

For this reason I have yet to actually play a match because I wanted to play it with my friends, not on my own. So I can't speak for any of the actual online gameplay.

The only reason I have not refunded the game yet is hope that they will fix this launch mess soon. Can't say it's worth the price as of right now.

Very fun game. Easy to learn, hard to master. Are there currently (June 23rd 2025) bugs, matching issues and/or lag? Yes. However, the developer has been rolling out near-constant fixes.

Look, do you like the idea of the game? Buy it. Practice, play through the hiccups.

Are you one of those people that expect no bugs in a game? Wait for more patching.

Are you one of those people that can turn the tide of a 5v5 match by taking the ball out from goal, juking and dancing, breaking ankles and scoring? Trick question, there are no players like that because the ones that think they can do it are actually garbage and need to learn teamwork.

Find space, get in space, pass the ball. Be a team player.

Thanks for reading and have a great rest of your day.

EDIT 8/5/2025:
I am changing my review after 100+ hours to Not Recommended. As many others have said:
- The game is still very buggy - the ippy slide, golden boost, an immense amount of desyncs, priority is really poor right now, etc.
- The community is toxic and there's no report button, so enjoy when your teammate wants to grief by scoring own goals until the mercy rule kicks in.
- Full control remapping is still unavailable
- Defensive maneuvers don't work in some cases, you will do a standing tackle and step right through the other player
-  Devs mentioned crossplay at launch then said it will be September at the minimum for crossplay

I could go on but I won't. The core of the game is there and it's really fun when it works but this game needed at least another 6 months in the oven. It's shocking that after the VERY BUGGY beta Sloclap didn't knock out some major bugs.

I won't write this game off yet, give it another 6 months and 2 or 3 more patches to get where it needs to be. Thanks for reading and have a quality day. TEKKERS!

O jogo é bom e divertido, mas precisa urgentemente corrigir uma série de problemas. Outra coisa, o jogo ainda não tem crossplay, o que pode ser bem ruim para algumas pessoas que gostariam de jogar com seus amigos. Se você quer muito jogar Rematch agora, compra e se divirta, mas eu aconselho esperar um pouco para ver se pelo menos alguns bugs serão concertados.

"Solid base with room to grow – great promise!"

I’m really enjoying Rematch – it’s fast-paced, competitive, and offers solid mechanics that grabbed me from the start. That said, it feels like it was rushed out of the oven a bit too soon. Comparing it to Rocket League just isn’t fair: Rocket League is the second chapter in a long-established IP, while Rematch is only getting started with its very first release.

That said, the game really needs more work on sound design. The effects feel muted and uninspired; they don’t land with impact when a play is made. And don’t get me started on the absence of a soundtrack – it’s practically deafening. Adding a dynamic score and beefier audio effects would massively boost the immersion.

There are also occasional desync issues, which can really throw off competitive play — hopefully that’s on the dev team’s radar.

In short, Rematch has a strong foundation and great mechanics. With better polish — especially in audio — it could easily stand out on its own. I’m looking forward to seeing how it evolves!

《师傅》的开发商Sloclap又给玩家们献新的大礼，看起来和《师傅》的动作元素差异甚大的足球竞技游戏《Rematch》

第一次在2024年的TGA上看到这款游戏的宣传片时相当惊讶，目前的足球游戏，不对，甚至可以说目前几乎所有市面上的体育竞技游戏的历代更新表现的都有些同质化，小修小补就是新的一部作品，买断制后无底洞般的一年一次内购抽卡更给人一种捞一波就跑路的丑恶既视感，而受众玩家限于这些年货作品“垄断”式的蝎子粑粑独一份也没办法抗拒

在这种情况下，Sloclap这个法国的中型团队如何从没有更多竞争和多余蛋糕的市场中分杯羹让人期待，或者说，他们能如何在足球竞技这个规则固化的机制内做到创新以形成有效竞争力

事实证明，《Rematch》做到了，相比于那些传统足球游戏，《Rematch》的强烈街头足球氛围赋予游戏一种足球竞技游戏本应具备的激情和热烈感，富有色彩对比度的非写实风格给予创作者更自由的想象力让画面表现的冲击力大大增强，也正因为如此，在宣传之初，《Rematch》就被期待的评价为“功夫足球”

但功夫足球的评价显然并不止步于画面风格，《Rematch》的玩法规则也显示出一种不受拘束的自由洒脱，没有传统意义上的越位等概念，甚至于守门员都可以跑出位置来个华丽丽的滑铲，而也因为着游戏的人数例如5V5远远少于现实中的足球比赛，导致游戏的节奏更加激烈紧快，人员的轮换非常重要，可以说，就像是在禁区内的守门员位置也并不固定，每个人都可以成为守门员，这给了玩家的战术安排更多的选择性

目前《Rematch》处于赛季0的阶段，作为一款买断制的赛季式长期运营的多人竞技足球游戏，它的基本盘已打磨的足够扎实，也凭借着讨巧的、有创新度的风格在市场中获得许多玩家的青睐。而在未来，它要如何进行持续运营，未来几个赛季会推出什么样的内容，或许是我们更值得期待的部分

Le jeu est génial , mais comme d'habitude on voit clairement qu'il est sorti beaucoup trop tôt. Une optimisation lamentable , des bugs , des crashs . Le contenu actuel c'est ranked ou casu , pas de tournois pas de ligues pas de championnats pas de modes de jeux fun pas de système de club. Les menus sont fades . Même pas un bouton réessayer dans les entraînements , il faut revenir au menu , retourner dans l'entrainement et relancer, l'impression d’être sur un jeu qui a 20 ans de ♥♥♥♥♥♥ . Pas de crossplay non plus, et les touches ne sont pas personnalisables pleinement . C'est vraiment dommage car le jeu est vraiment fun ( quand on joue entre potes, la solo q est à éviter comme tout les jeux multi compétitifs )  mais tout le reste a coté est naze . Le seul truc cool à l'heure actuelle c'est le prix du jeu . Je fais confiance à Sloclap pour la suite mais y'a vraiment beaucoup beaucoup de boulot .

I played the beta and honestly, I’m still impressed. At first glance, Remacht might look a bit repetitive – but don’t be fooled. Every overtime scenario is tense, unpredictable, and gets your pulse going.

The gameplay strikes a great balance: easy to pick up, hard to master. There’s a clear skill gap, but it never feels punishing. Whether you're playing casually or coordinating tactically with friends, it just works – and it’s a ton of fun either way.

The mix of strategy, fast reactions, and teamwork creates some really intense and rewarding moments. And when you pull off a smart play or clutch a match, it feels amazing.

The community is great, and the devs are active and genuinely care about feedback. You can really see the potential this game has if it keeps going in the current direction.

If you're into tactical team-based games with high replayability – this one's for you.

Would Recommend - 10/10

Cool game, nice idea for mechanics and good vision for a soccer/fútbol game. Horribly executed as half the time the mechanics don't work like they are intended too. Most of the time, servers are horrible. You will get games with constant high ping and the game is pretty unplayable, ball teleportation away from you, you teleport from the ball, its a hit or miss. I do get games where I have solid ping, but way too frequent do I get high ping games when I'm only playing us-west. Love to play it when its playable and everyone in the game is actually playing, but that hardly happens anymore. I understand new games come with problems and that the devs have addressed the sever issues, but I just don't think the consumer should have to suffer issues that should have been fixed before the game was full released. They had plenty of time and it still persists. Overall I give the game a 5/10 right now but I see the potential and work being put in, but that doesn't outweigh all the downsides to playing right now.

If you take Rematch for what it is and don't take it too seriously, it's good fun. I played a fair bit in the beta and it was enough to make me purchase the game without hesitation.

There are a few quality of life improvements as well as a couple of drawbacks that I would hope the Devs would look to fix in the early days of the games release.

Some of the interactions between players such as when multiple players slide tackle into the ball doesn't seem consistent. I'm also not a big fan of the second or two stun lock when losing the ball, I think this can be tinkered a bit.

There are also ways to spam and abuse skills to continually juggle the ball which not only looks ridiculous, is just too easy to abuse.

One of the biggest downsides of the game, which to be honest isn't a fault with the game itself as you can apply this logic to any game where people that don't play well with others are forced to do so, is bad teammates. You know the type, refuse to pass, try to do skills every time they get the ball resulting in them losing the ball 9 times out of 10, spamming "well done" when any of their teammates make a mistake, refusing to go in goals when it's their turn and trying to dribble the length of the field etc etc. As I say, it's the usual suspects that don't play well with others and try to spoil the fun in any gaming community unfortunate enough to attract them. I assume it won't be as much of an issue in ranked matches but not everyone will want to try hard the game so some form of in game reporting and banning system for players that go out their way to ruin matches for everyone else would be a good step to rid the community of bad eggs.

The mini-games don't have a quick restart option which again, from a quality of life point of view where players want to jump back in immediately, this has to be implemented as opposed to exiting out, re-selecting the challenge and having to wait for it to load back up.

Imo it's a bold move to not go free to play or even dip in at a lower price but hopefully with some updates, Rematch will be able to retain a good player base and have some longevity. I had similar hopes for Knockout City which I think was a very underrated game, I hope that rematch doesn't have a similar outcome.

On a minor note, some of the achievements are buggy and don't unlock such as winning 3 games in a row which I've done in 3v3, 4v4 and 5v5. I've read some players are having issues with other achievements as well so a fix for these would be ideal sooner rather than later.

All in all, very good fun to be had, looking forward to more updates, content drops and quality of life improvements.

Rematch — это не просто ещё один футбольный симулятор, а настоящая революция в жанре. Студия Sloclap, известная по Sifu, создала нечто совершенно уникальное.

Ключевые особенности:

Уникальный геймплей. Управление только одним игроком с видом от третьего лица полностью меняет подход к тактике.

Свобода действий. Нет жёстких позиций, можно участвовать как в атаке, так и в обороне.

Динамичные матчи. Отсутствие пауз и фолов держит в напряжении от начала до конца.

Глубокие механики. Простые на первый взгляд элементы скрывают огромную глубину.

Плюсы игры:

Интуитивное управление. Быстрое освоение базовых механик.

Зрелищные моменты. Удары с лета, подкрутка мяча, пробросы создают невероятные моменты.

Командный дух. Форматы от 3×3 до 5×5 подчёркивают важность взаимодействия.

Справедливая монетизация. Только косметические предметы, без Pay-to-Win.

Минусы:

Технические проблемы. Проблемы с серверами, особенно в СНГ.

Ограниченный одиночный режим. Только тренировочные миссии.

Режим 5×5. Иногда слишком хаотичен.

Итог:
Rematch — это глоток свежего воздуха для жанра футбольных игр. Несмотря на некоторые шероховатости, игра предлагает увлекательный и уникальный опыт. Особенно понравится тем, кто устал от традиционных симуляторов и ищет что-то новое.

Рекомендация: если вы любите динамичные онлайн-игры и готовы к новому опыту — однозначно стоит попробовать. Но будьте готовы к некоторым техническим трудностям на старте.

Constantly freezing at random times for a whole second, whether in game, practice, menu. (Running a 4070). 

Too many trolls, Goalies just passing the ball purposely to other goalie, or people with their friends will only pass between them.

If you stand in or too close to goal and try to pass the ball as goalie, it's a point for the opposing team. 

I don't know who they designed the Practice matches for, but some of those are impossible without finding a sneaky way to gold star them. Speaking of practice, add a dang restart button instead of kicking me to the main menu to go through the options to select it again, win or lose. I'm there to practice not just play a mini game.

The tutorial felt lacking, I finished it jumped in a game and still had no clue to half of the controls, or how to properly use them.

You get penalized for leaving match (fair). (So just Alt + F4 instead, it wont penalize you)

It crashes mid game and you can't rejoin if it does.

Some of the moves feel like, sometimes it works, sometimes it doesn't only if it feels like it, the ball will just phase through you like you don't exist.

Fair price, new release, just needs a lot of little things fixed.

Análise: Jogo bom e divertido, no entanto, é mais legal quando se tem amigos para jogar junto e atualmente no game, tem diversos bugs na loja, rede, quando você altera o idioma do jogo, toda vez que você entra tem que alterar de novo... The idea is great, but the execution still needs some work. Defending is an actual nightmare. Since there are no offside rules, teams end up just pinging the ball around from goal keeper to goal keeper in hopes someone makes a mistake. Collision resolution result in constant visible rollback, and that results in quite bad and inconsistent experience.
The progression is none existent besides some achievements. The "free" battle pass (you gain access to with the purchase of the base game) doesn't really have anything interesting, but the dev to flaunt some nicer items before your eyes to get you to pay more to get access to premium level battle pass.
Settings menu is an actual minefield. Putting the game into fullscreen locks up my machine, and subsequent restarts result in the same outcome. There is also no way to switch what your main screen is. It's 2025. Many people have dual monitor setup these days.
In matchmaker it's better to try and play with a premade group, because otherwise it all devolves very quickly into a pile of players just chasing the ball around with everyone taking their turn trying to run through the other team to score. Also, they really use "matchmaker" term very loosely. There is really no matchmaker. It's just puts random people in teams as quickly as possible.
Might come back in a few months to see how this game is doing.

[h1] Rematchが正式リリースされて私が感じた事 [/h1]
私はただの日本に住んでる普通のゲーマー。
でも今日はついに、あのRematchが正式リリースされた。

このゲーム？
ガチで神。伝説級。開発者、前世で世界救ってるレベル。表彰状じゃ足りない、国宝にすべき。

リリースされた瞬間、手が勝手に動いた。
ダウンロード→起動→プレイ→廃人化。秒だった。

食事？忘れた。睡眠？そんなの二の次。
今の私にはRematchだけが栄養源。

今日？朝から晩まで、いや今もやってる
指は痛い、目は乾く、でも心は満たされてる。幸福ってこれなんだなって思った

あの頃、ベータ期間が終わって絶望してた自分に言いたい
「大丈夫、希望はあった。Rematchは帰ってきた」って

もう止まらない。寝ても覚めてもRematch。
課金？喜んで。命を預けるレベル。
これはゲームじゃない。呼吸。人生。魂の拠り所。

ここに誓う。
Rematchを日本中に広める。全力で布教する。
バズらせて、友達も家族も全員巻き込む所存。

Rematch無しの私？
お湯のないカップ麺。コントローラーのないPS5。
そんな人生、意味がない。あってはならない。

The core gameplay is very fun but this game should not have been released in the state it is. 

The controls take some getting used to but the game is incredibly satisfying once you've mastered them. When you get into lobbies where both teams are willing to play unselfishly and understand ball movement, the game scratches an itch that nothing else can. Queue times are pretty solid playing on US-east. I do not have the best wifi but I have not experienced any problems with lag that have tilted me to any degree.

Unfortunately, the game is incredibly buggy and frequently crashes. When trying to fix this problem, you will realize that hundreds of other players have the same complaint, but each uncovered a unique interaction that was causing their crash. As of writing this review. two of my six hours of playtime were from trying to diagnose the source of the game's instability. Some people say they had a problem until they uninstalled the beta from their computer. Some people say they had a problem until they deleted their runtime file. Some people say they had a problem until they downloaded a fresh version of C++. It's ridiculous how many failure points there are when the game had multiple beta periods. 


I encountered what seems to have been a unique bug where any application with an active overlay freezes the game. This includes AMD Radeon, Discord, the Steam Overlay, and even Spotify. This was not a problem with my hardware: tapping the windows key or using a shortcut to open task manager would cause the game to unfreeze for a couple of seconds at a time. When trying to force quit the not responding Rematch application the game somehow freezes my whole computer, to the point where Control Alt Delete does not work. The easiest way to deal with these freezes is by unplugging the hdmi cable from your computer, which causes the game's anti-cheat to instantly crash the application and return control of your PC. This was a frustrating problem that took forever to diagnose. 

I am lucky that the solution ended up being as simple as disabling the overlay on every application I could think of, but I am told the underlying problem is the game's easy anti cheat package and it's poor implementation.

I've played REMATCH for over 60 hours across the different betas. 

Sloclap have managed to create an arcade sports game built on the actual real life principles of football/soccer in a way that no other sports game has managed before. The balance between depth/control and instant accessibility is genuinely brilliant. Despite being essentially over-sized futsal, the manual control over every interaction your player has with the ball feels more rewarding, more engaging, and more like real football than FIFA or eFootball ever have. 

That being said, there's still pretty frequent network issues, NO CROSSPLAY at launch even between gamepass and steam users who are both on PC (insane), and I would have liked more default customization options. Making me play the game for 2 hours to get enough currency to unlock a basic hairstyle that I used for all of the beta is just lame. 

This game is special. I'm hoping with the successful launch we'll see improvements very quickly and support for a very long time. 

Congratulations to Sloclap and thanks for the free copy from the raffle <3

edit: 50 hours in, and a few observations: 

While the gameplay is tight, everything else feels kinda undercooked: collision errors are not uncommon, like a tackle going through the ball, or said ball going through keeper's hands. UI in general (there is no replay button for a challenge, for example. You have to go back to the main menu to replay one, or there's no rematch button after a match, to try and queue with the same opponents). The battle pass, that feels lackluster with the wait for next tier of rewards. There are also more major issues, like ranked being not really ranked, i.e. you can queue with a bronze dude being Elite rank, cause since there is no crossplay yet, they had to do this Band-Aid fix so players wouldn't suffer through huge wait times (which was already happening). 

With and despite ALL that in mind, here is my take:

All this makes me feel like the game should've released as early access, not a full game. In Brazil,  it costs 90 reais, a bit less than 20 usd. But my friend, in a US account, had to pay 30 bucks. Is it worth all that? It depends: if 30 is not going to set you back, and you have money to spare, go for it. If not, I would suggest maybe waiting for the game to be properly updated, or maybe buy Game Pass

Even so, the game is VERY fun. I'm having a blast, and can't wait to see the next steps, what the devs will cook. I'm really very happy with the product, I just hope they will give it the appropriate support in the coming months. And a proper ranked mode, asap.

A very refreshing and fun game, where there's a lot to master — but you can still enjoy it even if you're bad.
The game takes what's best in football (teamwork, goals, and fun combos) and mixes it with dynamic and satisfying gameplay. It's as simple as that. And when you play with friends, it triple the fun.

I’ll easily put hundreds of hours into this game. For the price of €24.99 (I paid €15), it’s totally worth it — there’s a lot to learn, and ranked multiplayer is always a challenge.

Since the game is already very good, I’ll focus on the negative aspects:

1 – It’s a team-based online multiplayer game, so like all of them, you’ll find people who don’t play as a team or who are toxic. Solo queue requires strong mental resilience — which many players don’t have.

2 – The monetization model might bother some people. It’s similar to Hunt: Showdown — a paid game with a battle pass and skins. Personally, I don’t mind (also most skin are easy to get):

- A paid game helps reduce cheaters,

- The battle pass supports new content,

- And skins help fund development.

3 – The game feels more like an early access or a beta.
There can be desyncs, bugs, and crashes. It doesn’t happen often, but it’s enough to be annoying for some players. The game need at least one more month of polish

⚽️ REVIEW DE REMATCH – Futebol online com alma de anime de porrada

Nota: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Horas jogadas: o suficiente pra desenvolver trauma digital

🎮 Você achou que era só mais um joguinho de futebol online? Errado.
Rematch é o caos estilizado onde reflexos, blefes e ataques psicológicos valem mais que tática.
Gráficos lisos e estilosos tipo Sifu, movimentação acrobática, e partidas que duram menos que um miojo — mas com o triplo da tensão.

✅ Prós:

Só online: ou seja, você só apanha de gente real com tempo livre e reflexo de ninja.

Visual absurdo de bonito: parece que Street Fighter e Rocket League fizeram um filho artista.

Cada gol é um highlight digno de TikTok e terapia.

Mecânica simples de aprender e difícil de dominar = perfeito pra humilhação pública.

❌ Contras:

Não tem IA: se não tiver internet, não tem goleada (e nem vingança).

Às vezes você entra achando que vai ganhar… e toma 5x0 em 30 segundos.

A frase "só mais uma" vira 2 da manhã.

💡 Melhor parte:
O botão “rematch” existe por um motivo. A vingança é uma mecânica central.

Resumo final:
Rematch é futebol de ação, com estilo, fluidez e pancadaria esportiva entre desconhecidos online.
Você entra pra se divertir e sai com sede de revanche, xingando um cara chamado “ZéGOL_2008”.

🎯 Veredito final:
Compre. Conecte. Perca. Reclame do lag. Dê rematch. Vença. Sorria.
5 estrelas porque não existe 6.

This game has the makings of something great. Fun mechanics, competitive nature, weird but okay character customization. There's so much promise here. HOWEVER! When you participate in playtests well before the game releases and every single issue from them is still there and more I have a problem. I mean simply write "We do not care about our customers" in the game description. I mean the games an absolute pain to play but they added a battlepass already?
1) The game runs absolutely terribly. I don't care what frames you set it at it won't run at that rate. 
2) The glitching and skipping and false obstructions AND the ball just plainly not connecting to your character make the game very difficult to play. 
3) The other team will just faze through you and take the ball
4) And this one is personally my least favorite, the input delay on every input is rather unplayable. 
I could go on and on but the main point is this: This game will probably be worth it when they fix each and every one of those issues. Until then, I wouldn't recommend it to anybody. {P.S. All of these things are things people I know have complained about as well it's not just my setup}

先说结论：2025年上半年玩过最好玩的游戏

  作为一个经常在现实中踢球的玩家，这游戏反倒比fifa那些更沉浸，也更符合我的胃口，很多竞技思路和脚法微操和现实中踢球的状态高度重合，我相信现实中也爱踢球的兄弟应该会有所体会。

  但如果你是fifa或实况的忠实玩家，这款游戏可能并不太适合你。甚至可以说不太像一个类型的游戏。个人认为rematch相比于传统足球类游戏，更像动作游戏或fps类。我感觉rematch与fifa的相关性或许还没有nba2k与fifa的相关性高。

 但要注意的是：

1.如果你经常用手柄踢fifa，那键位需要重新适应。但假如你经常用手柄玩fps游戏，那上手可能会很快，特别神奇。

2.说匹配不到人的朋友们，如果是大陆玩家，建议加速亚洲北部相关节点，匹配速度会快很多

3.游戏定价还是过高，即便我很爱这款游戏

  最后，抛开定价，只谈游戏。如果满分5分我会给4分，拿掉一分为看他之后的优化和更新。
  第一次写游评。因为好久没有过让我感到如此惊艳的竞技游戏了。我也相信这款游戏会越来越好吧，毕竟是做出了《师傅》这款独立小精品的工作室啊！

Rematch é um game com uma premissa muito boa e, inclusive, bem diferente dos jogos de futebol que costumamos ver por aí no mercado. É fácil e simples de jogar, as partidas são rápidas e dinâmicas, e achei o fluxo de gameplay bem divertido também.

Joguei as duas betas e posso dizer que essa última versão veio bem abaixo do esperado. Ainda existem muitos bugs e crashes que precisam ser corrigidos, além da ausência de uma opção para reportar jogadores, algo essencial para esse tipo de jogo (ninguém passa a bola!!!).

Outro ponto que me decepcionou foi a customização, que continua bem limitada. Poderiam ter adicionado novas opções de cores, estampas e logos, mas praticamente não houve evolução desde as versões beta. Parece que o foco foi totalmente direcionado para o conteúdo pago e o passe de capitão. Resumindo, o jogo poderia ser muito mais, falta detalhamento e polimento, parece que tudo foi feito as pressas.

O preço cheio do jogo é bem salgado. Eu recomendaria esperar uma promoção ou que eles melhorem esses pontos antes de comprar. No momento, não consigo me divertir jogando sozinho, principalmente por causa desses problemas. Prefiro jogar com os amigos.

[h1]8/10[/h1]
[table]
[tr]
	[th]Positives[/th]
	[th]Negatives[/th]
[/tr]
[tr]
	[td]✅ Amazing interpretation of football with a few exciting new twists
✅ Animations and models are gorgeous yet distinctly readable for gameplay
✅ Clever communication system that encourages teamwork amongst players
[/td]
	[td]❌   A little light on content; you’ll need to be very invested in the gameplay loop
❌  Server issues currently causing some very disruptive experiences
❌  Restrictive battle pass model in an already expensive game
[/td]
[/tr]
[/table]

Nobody was expecting Sloclap, a studio made famous by their martial-arts focused action games, to release a competitive multiplayer football game. The pivot from Sifu initially seems crazy, but I can understand why Sloclap felt they were up to the challenge. The studio has experimented with multiplayer before with Absolver and has created hundreds of athletic body animations. Still, Rematch is a very ambitious title, and thankfully, one that sticks the landing.

Rematch set out to create a fun and engaging football video game, and they’ve succeeded tremendously. An extremely strong core gameplay loop makes every session so exciting and captivating. However, there’s no denying Rematch is still a little rough around the edges with performance issues and feeling small for its pricing. Recommended for: Rocket League-esque game lovers. If you have 2~4 friends whom you play coop games with regularly. If you like short and fast paced games where your control skills really matter - but you don't get frustrated by the results easily.


Not Recommended for: If you get easily frustrated when random teammates screw up and you lose a match. If you don't like putting in hours of effort to raise your control skills.


Feels like Rocket League before it went down the gutter. As many has pointed out - there's still a lot of bugs and glitches, which is a shame as a fully released product - but I didin't encounter that much game breaking bugs besides some occasional crashed. (Had 3 crashes during 3 days) 

Also, on the main menu of the game it says the game just launched and they are open to suggestions for improvement which is I mean, it sounds cool but I think as a paid product this the game should have been more polished in and out. This is not an early access or beta and if you're going to charge us then you might as well have put more effort in stability, functions and QOL features. Seriously, you can't even rotate your character in the customization screen which I think is something verrrry basic. 

Anyhow, despite of all the flaws this game nails it at the most important thing - the gameplay. The way they designed the gameplay is just right on the spot - I really think they should never try to change it and break the balance that you have now. It's an easy to pick-up and play type of game and there are so many ways a match can play out and I'm looking forward to how the ball play will evolve in the future.

The developers Slocap have been able to address many of the launch issues of the game. Enough to make me want to flip my review to positive. Matchmaking is fast and creates fairer games. Many of the crash issues have been resolved.

There are still remaining issues in the game where it can be hard to understand the reason the ball went the way it did. You'll still see the ball zoom past goalies and players then magnetize back onto their hands or feet. Many times the game just robs you. Even today you'll just sometimes get on a server that is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ the bed. Toxicity is pretty rampant and there aren't any features to report or avoid players.

However, there are cool moments too and vibing with a team to work together can be really exciting. Faking the goalie and sinking one into the net feels pretty good. The animations are cool and hit hard. I would tentatively recommend it as a game to spend some time with if you like soccer or competitive PVP games like Rocket League. I hope Slocap continues to resolve the issues in the game.

I love this game, honestly I do. In fact I've been pouring hours into it  but I CANNOT recommend it right now to new players due to the barebones state it's in and also the INSANE amount of bugs and glitches. If you can get past these like myself and still enjoy it, pick it up but be aware these are very prominent issues that are all over this game. 

-Goalkeeper dropping the ball in net for an own goal (happens way too often and does it without a button press). this horrific bug has cost me multiple matches and one in overtime...
-Major de-sync and connectivity issues
-Crashes, server disconnects, or my absolute favorite where you join a late match and cannot do ANYTHING for input forcing you to close the game and take a loss. 
-Tackles and slides seem to be a 50/50 and a joke at times rewarding the player who just got tackled the ball still and sometimes even in better position then before.
-Goalkeeping is extremely inconsistent where sometimes the ball will go through the keeper or they win the slide animation but somehow the possession goes to the other team. 
-Wall play is just flat out way too overpowered and easy to score with and needs major tweaking
-Only one ranked mode instead of 3's or 4's which is just flat out dumb

The devs should just be honest and say this isn't a full fledged release and say as it is, this is early access and feels/plays like an early access game. I enjoy this game a ton and will gladly change this into a positive within a month or two when these things are ironed out but for right now, just wait till they fix these infuriating problems.
When you fail a practice mode, the game will only give you the option to go back to main menu. It would be great to also have the option to immediately restart the practice.

Game looks fun, great idea, but in the end it's horrible. The major issues are the community itself and the game not being finished at all. A lot of desync, bugs, glitches, and because of that the gameplay feels boring, frustrating and very clunky. Your teammates are your biggest enemies, not the other team. Nobody gives a pass or a cross, everyone is just trying to be Neymar or Messi, then they lose the ball and they don't even come to defend anymore, just waits in the opposing field for a pass so they would try to score. VERY ANNOYING. If you are not passing to them, or you make a mistake trying to build up the play, they mock you and start trolling. I think this game is made to be played by a full 3/4/5 premade, not solo/duo with a friend.
Every time i played with my friend, in every single match i was the goalkeeper at the begging. After a goal, he would be the second goalkeeper. WHY?. Until 2-0 or 1-1 (and this score to be achieved might take even the whole game) it's like we are playing solo, not together. I agree that everyone needs to be the goalkeeper but this rule is ♥♥♥♥. 80% of the players are designing their character to look gay, lesbian, trans, ladyboy, etc, some of you would say that i do not know how customization works in a multiplayer game, but in this one is just horrible, there is no place for this pride propaganda ♥♥♥♥ in football.
The gameplay itself is very frustrating. You slide tackle someone and they still have the ball, but if they slide tacke you, they're gonna get the ball no matter what. Same for the normal tackle, you do a perfect tackle but somehow they get pass you. The rainbow flick is too overpowered and very hard to defend when someone is just spamming that. The pass button is working like ♥♥♥♥, if you try to pass it to someone just use the shoot button, it's much better. 
You sprint for a ball and the keeper get's the ball in his hands outside the box, yeah... not frustrating at all. You sprint for a ball, the keeper runs outside the box to defend it, you dribble him, you shoot thinking no one is defending the net, but surprise, another player managed to get in the net and saves the ball. This happened almost in every game i played. And many more about the gameplay...
25€ for this ? Hell NAH. Thank GOD i didn't pay this much, but even for my 16€ spent i feel scammed. Maybe in 2-3 years or more, this game will be playable, but i won't give a second chance pretty soon. I don't even want to start on this one, with basic customization on your character that costs €, or the Ronaldinho skin which costs half of this full price game.

Very worth it!!! Super addicting soccer-based game play!! It's so amazing to finally be able to play third person soccer, without having to control more than one character! Watching videos and applying them in game is teaching me so much more about soccer, and I'm seeing so much positive benefits to my game sense in Rematch and other games too!

Mechanics:
The mechanics in this game allow for some very flashy game play, without having to sync many hours in freeplay. The shoot mechanic uses the crosshair in the middle of your screen to aim, so you can use it for far more than just shooting, such as big clears or long passes. The passing mechanic is really good, but does take some getting used to, since you normally have to aim the left stick the direction of the pass, which aims relative to the pitch, not your field of view.

The voice chat is really good, the positive emphasis in quick chat and in goal celebrations is very welcome.

Rough spots:
Currently there is not crossplay yet, but they are absolutely working on it. The other downside is some servers still are a bit laggy sometimes, or I've had to queue US-Central instead of US-West some days to get a game quickly. That said, the game is still really new so they are certainly attempting to iron out kinks more, and I've found this to be an issue in a very low percentage of games. Occasionally the game still gets a bit buggy, or even crashes, but this again has been a very low percentage of the time for me, since the games official release, and I'm sure the developers will keep smoothing out the rough spots.

I am hoping to see this game blow up in the Esports world!! It's so much fun to listen to and watch pro-level gameplay. Sometimes my low Elo games get rather quiet, at which times they usually don't play so great (yes I goof up sometimes too), but at least I can still give helpful callouts/suggestions in voice chat, and the game is still super new so a lot of people just don't know much yet; plus it's really nice that I jump over to chilling in some non-ranked 3v3 to work on various mechanics without concern when results don't go our way. Furthermore, I can't speak positively enough about some of the amazing youtube videos, that have given me so many VERY helpful insights to improve on my mechanics and role-based understanding. :)

Ich bin absolut begeistert von Rematch! Die Lernkurve ist zweifellos steil – das Spiel fordert einen von Anfang an, sowohl mechanisch als auch taktisch. Die Steuerung fühlt sich zu Beginn ungewöhnlich an, aber sobald man den Dreh raushat, entfaltet sich ein intensives, immersives Spielerlebnis, das stark an Rocket League erinnert – nur eben „mit Füßen“ statt Autos.

Gerade in den ersten Matches wirkt vieles chaotisch, doch mit etwas Übung lernt man, sich sinnvoll zu positionieren, clever zu passen und als Team zu agieren. Und genau da liegt der Reiz: Es ist kein reines Solo-Game. Man merkt schnell, dass Ego-Spielerei selten zum Erfolg führt. Zwar gibt es im Gelegenheitsmodus (Quickplay) immer wieder Spieler, die den Ball unbedingt alleine ins Tor tragen wollen, aber überraschend oft trifft man auf sehr kooperative Mitspieler – besonders wenn sie begriffen haben, wie wichtig Zusammenspiel ist.

Ein weiteres Thema, das ich ansprechen möchte: Manche stören sich an der Möglichkeit, den eigenen Charakter frei zu gestalten – inklusive LGBTQ+-freundlicher Ausdrucksformen. Für mich persönlich ist das absolut kein Problem, im Gegenteil: Ich finde es gut, dass Rematch hier Diversität zulässt. Es ist ein rein kosmetisches Feature und beeinträchtigt das Gameplay in keinster Weise.

Das Spiel hat meiner Meinung nach riesiges Potenzial – sowohl als Competitive-Titel als auch als Plattform für Community-Events. Ich hoffe sehr, dass Entwickler Sloclap in Zukunft regelmäßig neuen Content nachliefert – seien es neue Stadien, Spielmodi oder vielleicht sogar ein Editor für eigene Teams. (Hust ... Absolver lässt grüßen – bitte lasst Rematch nicht im Content-Durst verdursten.)

Kurz gesagt: Wer Lust auf ein sportliches, schnelles und ungewöhnlich tiefgehendes Teamspiel hat, sollte Rematch definitiv ausprobieren. Es ist fordernd, belohnend – und vor allem eines: richtig spaßig.



(Written with the help of ChatGPT, because my English isn’t perfect — but I want to reach as many people as possible.)

After almost 300 hours of playtime, I think it’s finally time to share my honest review. The game itself is wonderful in principle — it’s genuinely fun when you end up with a team that isn’t full of ego players. Those moments when teamwork actually happens are amazing.

But then there are the other matches — the ones where you can practically set your watch to when your teammate will lose the ball, just because they can’t stop showing off or running in circles. Honestly, I’ve seen it all by now: from the player who still doesn’t understand that the ball belongs in the enemy’s goal, to the one who refuses to realize that passing at the right moment can be the difference between scoring or losing the match.

I really believe if anti–teamplay behavior were punished more severely — like, really punished — the game would stay fun even when queuing with randoms.
Because if nothing changes about the community’s behavior, this game will end up being worse than League of Legends.

Reamatch, with its full release, has firmly established itself among competitive football games. The gameplay mechanics are solid, the match tempo is intense, and every match forces you to think strategically. After 25 hours of playtime, I’ve realized this game demands not just reflexes, but vision — it’s a true mental battle. At times, it genuinely feels like being in a Blue Lock scenario: evolve or disappear.

That said, there are still areas that need improvement. I encountered some technical issues, and the customization options are currently too limited. I’d love to see a deeper character creation system that allows players to reflect their own unique playstyle. Reamatch clearly has massive potential — this is just the beginning, and I believe it will reach an entirely new level in the coming year. I’ll be following future updates with great interest.

Rematch – Innovativ, rasant, aber noch mit Luft nach oben

Rematch ist ein erfrischend innovativer Titel, der es schafft, das Beste aus zwei Welten zu vereinen: Die Spielintensität von FIFA trifft auf die chaotisch-spaßige Dynamik sowie strategische Tiefe von Rocket League. Was daraus entsteht, ist ein interaktives Sportspiel, das nicht nur unterhält, sondern auch echte Skills und Teamwork erfordert.

Das Gameplay bietet überraschend viel Tiefe – von cleveren Passwegen bis hin zu taktisch abgestimmten Angriffen – hier ist deutlich mehr drin als nur wildes Herumgeklicke. Kommunikation, Positionierung und Timing entscheiden über Sieg oder Niederlage.

Trotz des gelungenen Konzepts wirkt das Spiel an einigen Stellen jedoch noch nicht ganz ausgereift. Das Passspiel fühlt sich teils unpräzise und hakelig an, die Server-Performance schwankt stark – gerade bei hitzigen Matches ist das frustrierend. Auch das aktuelle Ranked-System lässt zu wünschen übrig: Es fehlt an Feinschliff.
Ein großer Wunsch von mir: Ein 3vs3 und/oder 4vs4 Ranked-Modus, 

Fazit: Rematch ist ein interaktives, ideenreiches Sportspiel mit hohem Suchtfaktor, das frischen Wind in das Genre bringt. Mit ein wenig mehr Feinschliff bei Technik und Systemen hat das Spiel Potenzial.

Patch 1.200.300 Update Review: 

Honestly, after terrible patch after patch I've lost hope in this game. Maybe they'll make things better, but every patch is just one terrible decision after the next. Now with patch 1.200.300, they slowed movement down dramatically so you feel weighed down and you turn like a semi-truck. After multiple matches of the ball STILL going through me and ally goalies(one even costing me the match), and not being able to catch the ball because I see it coming  but can't move fast enough to receive it through the game's broken receiving, I've had enough. I'd refund if I could. 

Avoid this game for now.
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

It’s a GOOD game, but there’s two HUGE issues that currently make the game not worth playing.

1) The netcode is downright HORRENDOUS. Just AWFUL. People teleporting and skipping EVERY match, the ball teleporting into the goalkeeper’s hands, randomly slipping out your possession, ect. Just terrible. Multiple patches in, and it's still just as bad.

2) The ranked system is garbage. There’s 3 normal game modes, 3v3-4v4- and 5v5, but they choose 5v5 ranked ONLY because they were afraid to split the player base. They released a dev log saying we can expect ranked 4v4 and 3v3 in…. A few months…. Yeah…

The Ranked experience is..... bad. The game is fun to play, but I’ve genuinely seen Roblox games with better Ranked systems. Horrific lag aside(again), I’m so serious when I say this, I’ve genuinely had BRONZE ranked players on my team….. While in Gold. (I've even seen Plat+ be paired with Bronze players, it's just awful.)
Like most online games now, it’s based on SBMM. Well, they say it’s not SBMM, but it’s an SBMM adjacent matchmaking system…. That somehow doesn’t work. What could you compare the Ranked experience too?..... Think Marvel Rivals but in Soccer/Football form. It’s that awful. If you’re good, you will consistently get paired with people you NEED to carry, and if you’re bad you will get placed with people who carry you.

The handling of this game has been terrible so far. Everything from the loose matchmaking pairing, to the lack of Ranked variety, to the horrible netcode, to the awful action priority, it just seems like Slocap either has zero faith in their own game, or isn't equip for a game of this caliber.
